channel.title: Morning Mail
channel.description: Stay updated on the latest news and events happening in the RPI community.

item.title: Smoothie Socials
item.description: Date: Wednesday, May 6, 2026 10:00 AM - 11:00 AM - The Office of Alumni Engagement invites all students to join us on the first Wednesday of the month for RPI Smoothie Socials. Enjoy delicious smoothies and snacks, connect with fellow students and select staff members, and get to know the Alumni Engagement team. Each month features a fun theme, and a unique giveaway for the first 50 students through the door! May’s theme: Spring Fling. May’s Giveaway: RPI-branded sunglasses & color-changing…
item.author: Students

item.title: Residence Halls Close
item.description: Date: Saturday, May 9, 2026 12:00 PM - Residence halls will close at 12:00pm (noon) on May 9th. All residents will be expected to vacate their space by this time. Upon completion of an intersession housing form, graduating seniors may stay in their on-campus residence until 5:00pm, May 16th.
item.author: Students

item.title: Chemistry 1 and 2 Hype Squad
item.description: Date: Wednesday, May 6, 2026 7:35 AM - 8:05 AM - The FYE team will be stationed in front of Russell Sage Dining Hall to wish you well on your Chemistry 1 or Chemistry 2 exam! Swing by on your way to the exam for a mini pep rally and some sweet treats!
item.author: Students

item.title: Final Exam Review Sessions: Week of 4/29-5/6
item.description: Prepare for Final Exams with the Success Center Tutors! All Final Reviews will be at the Success Center. Please check-in at our front desk! -Strengths Final Review: Thursday 4/30 (5-7pm) -Thermo Final Review: Thursday, 4/30 (7-9pm) -CS I Final Review: Thursday, 4/30 (8-10pm) -Physics I Final Review: Friday, 5/1 (1-3pm) -Cal II Final Review: Friday, 5/1 (3-5pm) -MAU Final Review: Sunday, 5/3 (1-3pm) -MANE 1010 Essence of Eng. Final Review: Sunday 5/3 (1-3pm) -Eng. Probability Final Review:…
item.author: Students

item.title: Commencement Important Dates and Details
item.description: On Monday, April 27th important dates and items were sent out to all registered graduates attending commencement. Keep an eye out for all communications as graduation day approaches!
item.author: Students

item.title: Sherry Road lot changes
item.description: Effective Monday, May 4, Sherry Road lot will transition to pay per hour parking only. Please ensure your parking session is active for the duration of your stay, as vehicles without valid payment may be subject to enforcement. For additional information, please contact Parking and Transportation Services.
item.author: Community

item.title: Parking Lot Closure – Crane Staging (May 7)
item.description: Please be advised that, due to an HVAC project, a crane will be staged in the G-Lot on May 7. The lot (Commons Dining Hall, softball field, Mueller Center, and DPS areas) will be closed and barricaded starting the evening of May 6 for approximately 24 hours. Pedestrians should use caution due to construction activity.
item.author: Community

item.title: President’s Commencement Colloquy
item.description: Date: Friday, May 15, 2026 3:30 PM - Join us for our annual President’s Commencement Colloquy, a lively conversation with our Commencement honorary degree recipients, The Honorable Dr. Darío Gil and Christine Ann Miller ’97 — two remarkable leaders at the forefront of science and health care. Doors open at 3 p.m. Colloquy begins at 3:30 p.m. and will be followed by a reception with refreshments in Evelyn’s Café. Free and open to the public. Registration required.
item.author: Community

item.title: Monumental Death
item.description: April 16-May 8, 2026 How are we implicated in the images of power that surround us? How do our actions mimic what we have learned from those that lead us? Installation Kate Ladenheim
item.author: Community

item.title: Graduate Students Needed to Solve National Security Problems
item.description: Earn credits this fall as you join teams to solve real problems presented by the Department of Defense. Students from all schools, including MBA students, are encouraged to register the fall Hacking 4 Defense course. Reach out to Prof. Clint Ballinger at ballic2@rpi.edu for more information. All disciplines are welcome and encouraged to register.
item.author: Community

item.title: Division of Human Resources Workshop: RPI MAX for Staff: Leveraging Feedback and Career Opportunities- May 7, 2026 8AM-10AM
item.description: Date: Thursday, May 7, 2026 8:00 AM - 10:00 AM - As an employee, you work in partnership with your manager to align expectations and set meaningful goals. Throughout the year, you check in together, recalibrate as needed, and celebrate progress and achievements. This workshop focuses on supporting your individual career development, preparing for the annual manager evaluation, setting effective goals, and using tools that can help you advance your career.
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Division of Human Resources Workshop: RPI MAX for Supervisors: Performance and Coaching - May 7, 2026 10AM-12PM
item.description: Date: Thursday, May 7, 2026 10:00 AM - 12:00 PM - As a manager, you play a central role in guiding the performance process for your employees—working collaboratively to align expectations, support development, and monitor progress toward achieving goals. This workshop introduces the core principles of performance coaching, including how to identify and evaluate performance gaps, set effective goals, and lead the RPI MAX performance process. Participants will also explore practical tools for…
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: MBA for Professionals Review with RPI at Work on May 6
item.description: Date: Wednesday, May 6, 2026 12:00 PM - 12:30 PM - Learn to lead from every aspect of an organization with a real-world MBA experience. Prepare to lead the most dynamic and innovative organizations. Engage, involve, motivate, and lead others to reach their fullest potential. Join RPI Dean Aric Krause, Ph. D., as he reviews this innovative project-based, online MBA for professionals program. Please register below to receive login details to join this virtual event.
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Training: Handshake for Faculty/Staff
item.description:
Are you a faculty or staff member hiring for on-campus research or student assistant positions? Consider using Handshake as a recruiting tool! The CCPD will host a training session about using the system on Wednesday, May 20th from 12-12:45pm in their office, DCC 209. Advanced sign-up is required; email Nicole Doran (stongn@rpi.edu) before Wednesday, May 13th to register or learn more!

For information about sharing your news or events, or to change your subscription settings, visit morningmail.rpi.edu.
item.author: Faculty and Staff
